islands.name: Asset Island
islands.description: Learn about stocks, bonds, ETFs, and mutual funds on this bustling financial hub.

learning_objectives: Understand stocks, bonds, ETFs, and mutual funds
Learn portfolio diversification and risk management
Compare asset classes by volatility, yield, and fees
provenance.originality_notes: Theme: bustling island marketplace (not a trading floor simulator). Verb: NPC-guided quests + paper-trading minigame (not pure day-trading). Structure: sequential quest unlocks teaching one concept at a time (not open-ended trading). Blends adventure exploration with simplified market mechanics.

areas.name: Market Street
areas.description: Bright ticker screens line the streets. Traders hustle between stalls.

areas.name: Broker Hall
areas.description: A grand marble hall where brokers execute trades. Practice paper trading here.

areas.name: News Stand
areas.description: Newspapers and screens everywhere. News events move the markets.

areas.name: ETF Research Lab
areas.description: Quiet desks with data terminals. Compare funds and pick the best one.

items.name: Trading License
items.description: Penny gave you this license to enter Broker Hall.

items.name: Portfolio Report
items.description: Proof that you successfully built a diversified paper portfolio.

items.name: Bond Certificate
items.description: Earned by understanding the difference between bonds and stocks.

items.name: ETF Detective Badge
items.description: Awarded for correctly matching ETFs to investor goals.

items.name: Asset Island Medallion
items.description: The ultimate proof of financial asset mastery. Unlocks the next island!

quests.title: Portfolio Starter
quests.description: Learn diversification by building a starter portfolio with virtual money.
quests.hint: Talk to Penny on Market Street to get your Trading License, then visit Marcus in Broker Hall.

quests.title: Bonds vs Stocks
quests.description: Understand volatility, yield, and how news events move markets.
quests.hint: Head to the News Stand and talk to Nina the Analyst.

quests.title: ETF Detective
quests.description: Compare ETFs on fees, holdings, and sector exposure. Pick the best match for each goal.
quests.hint: Enter the ETF Research Lab (requires Bond Certificate) and talk to Dr. Fund.

nodes.speaker: Penny the Trader
nodes.text: Welcome to Market Street! I'm Penny. Want to learn how to trade stocks, bonds, and more?

nodes.speaker: Penny the Trader
nodes.text: Great! First rule: diversification. Don't put all your eggs in one basket. Take this Trading License — it'll get you into Broker Hall where Marcus can set up your paper trading account.

nodes.speaker: Penny the Trader
nodes.text: Head to Broker Hall — it's connected from here. Marcus will show you the trading simulator. Good luck!

nodes.speaker: Penny the Trader
nodes.text: No problem! Come back when you're ready to start. The markets will wait.

nodes.speaker: Marcus the Broker
nodes.text: Ah, you have a Trading License! Ready to try paper trading? You'll get $10,000 in virtual cash to build a portfolio over 30 simulated trading days.

nodes.speaker: Marcus the Broker
nodes.text: A portfolio is your collection of investments — stocks, bonds, etc. The key is spreading your money across different asset types so one bad day doesn't wipe you out. That's diversification!

nodes.speaker: Nina the Analyst
nodes.text: Markets move on news. Bonds and stocks react very differently to the same headlines. Want to see how?

nodes.speaker: Nina the Analyst
nodes.text: A bond is like an IOU. You lend money to a company or government, and they pay you back with interest. Bonds are usually less risky than stocks but offer lower returns. Ready to compare them?

nodes.speaker: Dr. Fund
nodes.text: Welcome to the ETF Research Lab! An ETF — Exchange-Traded Fund — bundles many assets together so you can invest in a whole market with one purchase. Want to become an ETF Detective?

choices.text: What makes ETFs different from stocks?

nodes.speaker: Dr. Fund
nodes.text: Let me pull up the comparison terminal…

nodes.speaker: Dr. Fund
nodes.text: When you buy a stock, you own a piece of one company. An ETF is like a basket — it holds shares in many companies. You get instant diversification! But watch the fees — they eat into your returns over time.

nodes.speaker: Old Timer Walt
nodes.text: Been in markets for 40 years, kid. Want some free advice?

choices.text: What's the biggest mistake beginners make?

nodes.speaker: Old Timer Walt
nodes.text: Rule #1: Time in the market beats timing the market. Don't try to predict when to buy and sell. Just invest regularly, stay diversified, and let compound growth do the work.

nodes.speaker: Old Timer Walt
nodes.text: Panic selling. Markets drop 20-30% sometimes — it's normal. Beginners sell at the bottom and miss the recovery. If you have a long time horizon, stay the course.

minigames.name: Paper Trading Simulator
minigames.description: Build a diversified portfolio with $10,000 virtual cash over 30 trading days.

minigames.name: Bonds vs Stocks Challenge
minigames.description: React to news events by choosing bonds or stocks. Learn volatility vs yield.

minigames.name: ETF Detective
minigames.description: Compare ETFs and pick the best match for each investor goal.

minigames.name: Don't Break the Basket
minigames.description: Allocate your coins across cash, bonds, index funds, stocks, and crypto — then weather live market years. Feel why spreading risk wins.

minigames.name: Compound Snowball
minigames.description: Reinvest your gains each year and watch the curve balloon, or cash out and stay flat. Compounding, learned by doing.
